days.title: Sonar Sweep
days.essence: Count how many entries in a list of depths exceed the previous one; part 2 does the same over a sliding window of three. Pure warmup to learn the I/O format.

days.title: Dive!
days.essence: Fold a list of forward/up/down commands into a position; part 2 adds an 'aim' term so depth accumulates from forward moves. Trivial state tracking.

days.title: Binary Diagnostic
days.essence: Derive gamma/epsilon from the most/least common bit in each column; part 2 iteratively filters candidates by bit-criteria until one remains. Column-wise bit counting plus a filtering loop.

days.title: Giant Squid
days.essence: Mark drawn numbers across several bingo boards and detect when a row or column fills; part 1 wants the first winner, part 2 the last. Straightforward board bookkeeping with mild multi-block parsing.

days.title: Hydrothermal Venture
days.essence: Rasterize line segments onto a grid and count cells covered by 2+ lines; part 1 is orthogonal lines only, part 2 adds the 45-degree diagonals. Drawing lines via integer steps and counting overlaps.

days.title: Lanternfish
days.essence: Exponentially growing fish population modelled by counting fish per timer bucket rather than per individual; the solution builds a DP table of descendants per starting timer. Part 2 (256 days) makes per-fish simulation impossible, forcing the counting/DP insight.

days.title: The Treachery of Whales
days.essence: Find the position minimizing total fuel to align all crabs; part 1's optimum is the median, part 2 uses triangular (1+2+...) costs near the mean. A 1D convex cost minimization (here found by hill-climbing from the median).

days.title: Seven Segment Search
days.essence: Part 1 counts digits with unique segment counts (1,4,7,8); part 2 deduces the scrambled wiring of every digit by segment-overlap logic. The difficulty is the deductive elimination that uniquely identifies each digit.

days.title: Smoke Basin
days.essence: Find low points on a heightmap (part 1), then measure the basins around them via flood fill bounded by height-9 ridges (part 2). Grid neighbor analysis plus connected-region flood fill.

days.title: Syntax Scoring
days.essence: Validate nested bracket strings with a stack: part 1 scores the first illegal closer (corruption), part 2 scores the completion needed for incomplete lines. Classic stack-based balanced-delimiter parsing.

days.title: Dumbo Octopus
days.essence: Energy levels rise each step and a cell at >9 flashes, cascading +1 to its eight neighbors until stable; part 1 counts flashes after 100 steps, part 2 finds the first all-synchronized step. Grid simulation with chain-reaction flash propagation.

days.title: Passage Pathing
days.essence: Count all start-to-end paths through a cave graph where small caves may be visited once; part 2 allows exactly one small cave to be revisited. DFS path enumeration with a visited-set constraint.

days.title: Transparent Origami
days.essence: Fold a sheet of dots along given axes by reflecting coordinates and deduplicating; part 1 counts dots after one fold, part 2 reveals a letter pattern after all folds. Coordinate reflection with a final ASCII-art read-off.

days.title: Extended Polymerization
days.essence: Pair-insertion grows a polymer exponentially, so track counts of each adjacent pair rather than the literal string. Part 2 (40 steps) blows up the string length, forcing the pair-counting trick over naive expansion.

days.title: Chiton
days.essence: Find the lowest-total-risk path across a weighted grid using Dijkstra; part 2 tiles the grid 5x5 with incrementing risk, enlarging the search 25-fold. Textbook Dijkstra on a grid.

days.title: Packet Decoder
days.essence: Decode a hex transmission into a recursively nested BITS packet tree honoring literal vs operator and both length-type encodings; part 1 sums versions, part 2 evaluates the expression tree. The challenge is the careful recursive descent over a bitstream.

days.title: Trick Shot
days.essence: Launch a probe under gravity/drag so it lands in a target box; part 1 maximizes peak height, part 2 counts all velocities that hit. Solvable by reasoning about the ballistic arithmetic or brute-forcing a bounded grid of initial velocities.

days.title: Snailfish
days.essence: Add nested pair 'numbers' and repeatedly reduce via explode (carry to nearest left/right leaf) and split until stable, then compute magnitude. The difficulty is the fiddly tree manipulation with neighbor propagation; part 2 maximizes magnitude over all ordered pairings.

days.title: Beacon Scanner
days.essence: Reconstruct a beacon map from scanner reports of unknown position and one of 24 orientations, aligning pairs that share at least 12 beacons. Heavy 3D rotation/translation matching; part 2 wants the largest Manhattan distance between scanners.

days.title: Trench Map
days.essence: Enhance an image by mapping each pixel's 3x3 neighborhood to a 9-bit index into a lookup table; the gotcha is that the infinite background can flip on/off each step. Part 1 enhances twice, part 2 fifty times.

days.title: Dirac Dice
days.essence: Part 1 is a trivial deterministic-die race; part 2's quantum die splits the universe on every roll, so you count wins over an astronomically branching tree by memoizing on (positions, scores). The insight is recursive counting with caching rather than enumerating universes.

days.title: Reactor Reboot
days.essence: Apply on/off instructions over 3D cuboids and count lit cubes; part 1 fits in a small 101^3 grid but part 2's huge coordinates demand cuboid intersection / inclusion-exclusion bookkeeping. The difficulty is splitting or signing overlapping boxes instead of voxel filling.

days.title: Amphipod
days.essence: Sort amphipods into their rooms for minimum energy, encoding the burrow as a state and running Dijkstra/A* over legal moves with intricate movement rules. Part 2 inserts two extra rows, enlarging the state space; the difficulty is correct move generation plus search.

days.title: Arithmetic Logic Unit
days.essence: Find the largest/smallest 14-digit number a given ALU program accepts; brute force is hopeless, so you must disassemble the input's repeated push/pop-on-base-26 structure and derive digit constraints. The puzzle is reverse-engineering your specific MONAD program.

days.title: Sea Cucumber
days.essence: Two herds move simultaneously each step (east herd first, then south) with wraparound; find the first step on which nothing moves. A simultaneous-update grid simulation; the day-25 finale is normally free once you have 49 stars, but here it was left unsolved.
